tex:
**[2024 May/June, 0580/31, Question 1]**

(a) The cost of a cinema ticket is \$6.30.

(i) (b) Work out the total cost of 4 tickets.

(c) The cinema sells 10 tickets for the price of 9 tickets.
A group of 10 people go to the cinema.
Work out how much each person must pay.

(d) The cinema has 650 seats.

(i) (e) There are 26 seats in each row.
Work out the number of rows.

(f) 84\% of the seats are occupied.
Work out how many seats are not occupied.

(g) Suki buys a bag of popcorn and a bottle of water.
A bag of popcorn costs twice as much as a bottle of water.
She receives \$3.55 change from \$10.
Work out the cost of a bag of popcorn and the cost of a bottle of water.

Bag of popcorn 
Bottle of water 

(h) (i) (i) A film lasts for 155 minutes.
Write 155 minutes in hours and minutes.



(j) Another film starts at 14 45 and lasts for 2 hours and 20 minutes.
Work out the time that this film finishes.

(k) The table shows the hours that Trevor works in the cinema each week.

Monday

No hours

Tuesday

No hours

Wednesday

18 00 to 22 00

Thursday

18 00 to 22 00

Friday

12 00 to 17 30

Saturday

12 00 to 17 30

Sunday

13 00 to 18 00

Trevor is paid \$11.50 per hour for the time he works before 18 00.
He is paid 30\% more for the time he works after 18 00.
Work out how much Trevor is paid each week.

(a) 
t Chen asks some people if they prefer a beach, cruise, lake or mountain holiday.

The pie chart shows the results.

(i)  Find the fraction of people who prefer a mountain holiday.

Give your answer in its simplest form.

 Find the percentage of people who prefer a beach holiday.

 Find the ratio of people who prefer each type of holiday in the form

beach : cruise : lake : mountain.
Give your answer in its simplest form.

 One person is chosen at random.

Find the probability that this person prefers a cruise or a lake holiday.

 675 people prefer a beach holiday.

Show that the total number of people Chen asks is 1800.

\begin{subsubparts}

\subsubpart Complete the table.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
Type of holiday & Pie chart sector angle & Frequency \\
\hline
Beach & $135\degree$ & 675 \\
\hline
Cruise & $30\degree$ & 150 \\
\hline
Lake & $75\degree$ & \\
\hline
Mountain & $120\degree$ & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\subsubpart Complete the bar chart, including the scale on the frequency axis.

\end{subsubparts}

t Mr Gibb pays \$2208 for a holiday.

Mr Shah pays 2050 euros for the same holiday.

The exchange rate is 1 euro = \$1.15\,.

Work out how much more, in euros, Mr Shah pays for the holiday than Mr Gibb.
texHtml:
(a) t Chen asks some people if they prefer a beach, cruise, lake or mountain holiday. The pie chart shows the results. (i) Find the fraction of people who prefer a mountain holiday. Give your answer in its simplest form. Find the percentage of people who prefer a beach holiday. Find the ratio of people who prefer each type of holiday in the form beach : cruise : lake : mountain. Give your answer in its simplest form. One person is chosen at random. Find the probability that this person prefers a cruise or a lake holiday. 675 people prefer a beach holiday. Show that the total number of people Chen asks is 1800. \begin{subsubparts} \subsubpart Complete the table.
Type of holidayPie chart sector angleFrequency
Beach$135°$675
Cruise$30°$150
Lake$75°$
Mountain$120°$
\subsubpart Complete the bar chart, including the scale on the frequency axis. \end{subsubparts} t Mr Gibb pays \$2208 for a holiday. Mr Shah pays 2050 euros for the same holiday. The exchange rate is 1 euro = \$1.15\,. Work out how much more, in euros, Mr Shah pays for the holiday than Mr Gibb.

tex:
(a) 
t Here is part of the timetable for trains from Hinton to Jarmouth.

All trains take the same time to travel from Hinton to Jarmouth.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
Hinton & 10\,47 &  \\
\hline
Jarmouth & 11\,15 & 12\,35 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Complete the timetable.

 Marge arrives at Hinton station exactly 20 minutes before the 10\,47 train leaves.

Complete the clock diagram to show the time she arrives at Hinton station.

t Each day, a bus leaves Texford to travel to Cranbrook every 45 minutes.

The first bus leaves Texford at 07\,10.

The last bus leaves Texford at 22\,10.

Work out the number of buses that travel from Texford to Cranbrook each day.

t The cost of a bus pass increases every year.

On 1st January 2022 a bus pass costs \$50.

On 1st January 2023 the cost of the bus pass increases by 10\%.

On 1st January 2024 the cost of the bus pass increases by 5\%.

Calculate the cost of the bus pass on 1st January 2024.

t The Venn diagram shows information about the number of workers in a hotel who travel to work by bus ($B$) and train ($T$).

(i)  Work out the number of workers in the hotel.

 Work out $\text{n}(B \cup T)$.

 Explain in words what the number 85 in the Venn diagram represents.

 One of the workers is chosen at random.

Find the probability that this worker travels to work by bus and train.

t The hotel has single and double bedrooms in the ratio single : double $= 3 : 8$.

There are 75 more double rooms than single rooms.

Work out the number of single rooms.

The last bus leaves Texford at 22\,10. Work out the number of buses that travel from Texford to Cranbrook each day. t The cost of a bus pass increases every year. On 1st January 2022 a bus pass costs \$50. On 1st January 2023 the cost of the bus pass increases by 10\%. On 1st January 2024 the cost of the bus pass increases by 5\%. Calculate the cost of the bus pass on 1st January 2024. t The Venn diagram shows information about the number of workers in a hotel who travel to work by bus ($B$) and train ($T$). (i) Work out the number of workers in the hotel. Work out $\text{n}(B \cup T)$. Explain in words what the number 85 in the Venn diagram represents. One of the workers is chosen at random. Find the probability that this worker travels to work by bus and train. t The hotel has single and double bedrooms in the ratio single : double $= 3 : 8$. There are 75 more double rooms than single rooms. Work out the number of single rooms.

tex:
The stem-and-leaf diagram shows the ages of the 16 workers in a shop on 1st January 2022.

\begin{tabular}{r|l}
2 & 3 \quad 5 \quad 7 \\
3 & 4 \quad 6 \quad 6 \quad 6 \quad 9 \\
4 & 1 \quad 1 \quad 7 \\
5 & 0 \quad 7 \quad 7 \quad 8 \\
6 & 1 \\
\end{tabular}

Key: $2 \mid 5$ represents 25
(a) 
t
(i)  Work out the range.

 Write down the mode.

 Work out the median.

 Work out the percentage of workers that are older than 40 but younger than 60.

t On 1st January **2023** the shop has the same 16 workers.

Write down the range, the mode and the median on 1st January **2023**.

t On 1st January **2024** the oldest worker leaves.

His replacement is a new worker born on 12th November 1970.

There are no other changes to the workers.

Complete the stem-and-leaf diagram for 1st January 2024.

\begin{tabular}{r|l}
2 & 5 \quad 7 \quad 9 \\
3 & 6 \quad 8 \quad 8 \quad 8 \\
4 & \\
5 & \\
6 & \\
\end{tabular}

Key: $2 \mid 5$ represents 25

t The shop owner is $x$ years old.

$x$ is a prime number.

$x + 2$ is a square number.

$x - 2$ is a multiple of 9.

Find the value of $x$.

t The scale drawing shows the position of a castle, $C$.

The scale is 1 centimetre represents 2 kilometres.

Micah walks at 4.8\,km/h for 3 hours on a bearing of $236\degree$ from $C$, to his house, $H$.

Mark the position of $H$ on the scale drawing.

t A restaurant, $R$, is on a bearing of $083\degree$ from $C$.

(i)  Work out the bearing of $C$ from $R$.

 The distance, $d$\,km, from $R$ to $C$ is 5\,km, correct to the nearest kilometre.

Complete this statement about the value of $d$.

...\ $\leqslant d <$ ...

t A model of the castle is made.

On the model, the length of one of the castle walls is 5 centimetres.

The actual length of the wall is 90 metres.

Find the scale of the model in the form $1 : n$.

t The castle has two kitchens with rectangular floors.

Rectangle $ABCD$ is mathematically similar to rectangle $EFGH$.

Calculate the value of $x$.

t The castle has a cylindrical tower.

The tower has a radius of 2.4\,m and a height of 25\,m.

Calculate the curved surface area of the tower.

Give the units of your answer.

The table shows some values for $y = x^2 - x - 3$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-3$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $1$ & $2$ & $3$ & $4$ \\
\hline
$y$ & & & $-1$ & & & $-1$ & & $9$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) 
t Complete the table.

t On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - x - 3$ for $-3 \leqslant x \leqslant 4$.

t Write down the coordinates of the lowest point on the graph.

t Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - x - 3 = 7$.
